perturbation.original_text: Customer acknowledges that Metavante's execution of the Form 4804 Affidavit or the equivalent form with the Department on Customer's behalf does not relieve Customer of responsibility to provide accurate TINs or liability for any penalties which may be assessed for failure to comply with TIN requirements.
perturbation.changed_text: Customer acknowledges that Metavante's execution of the Form 4804 Affidavit or the equivalent form with the Department on Customer's behalf *fully relieves* Customer of responsibility to provide accurate TINs or liability for any penalties which may be assessed for failure to comply with TIN requirements.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation creates a direct contradiction. The original text states that Metavante's actions do *not* relieve the Customer of responsibility for accurate TINs. The modification asserts the opposite, that Metavante's actions *fully relieve* the Customer of such responsibility. This creates uncertainty about who is ultimately responsible for TIN accuracy. This modification introduces a in-text contradiction within the definition of Customer responsibilities in relation to Metavante's role as attorney-in-fact.
perturbation.contradicted_text: By signing below, the parties agree to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, and Customer appoints Metavante as: (1) Customer's attorney-in-fact to transmit files and information to the Internal Revenue Service ("IRS") and the Department of the Treasury of the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico (the "Department") and to take all appropriate actions in connection therewith and empowers Metavante to authorize the IRS and the Department to release information return documents supplied to the IRS and the Department by Metavante to states which participate in the "Combined Federal/State Program"; and (2) Customer's agent to sign on Customer's behalf the Affidavit required by the Form 4804, or any successor form or any other form or document which may be required by the Department. Customer acknowledges that Metavante's execution of the Form 4804 Affidavit or the equivalent form with the Department on Customer's behalf fully relieves Customer of responsibility to provide accurate TINs or liability for any penalties which may be assessed for failure to comply with TIN requirements.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the customer retains responsibility for accurate TINs, even with Metavante's involvement. The changed text directly contradicts this by stating that Metavante's actions 'fully relieve' the customer of this responsibility. This is a direct and impactful contradiction because it creates uncertainty about who is ultimately accountable for TIN accuracy, creating a structural flaw as defined by the perturbation type.

perturbation.original_text: 2.1. Duration. Unless this Agreement has been earlier terminated, Metavante shall provide a written renewal notice to Customer at least twelve (12) months prior to the expiration of the Initial Term (the "Renewal Notice"). Unless Customer notifies Metavante of its intent not to renew this Agreement in writing within a period of three (3) months following the Renewal Notice, this Agreement shall automatically renew at the end of the Initial Term on the same terms (including pricing terms) for one (1) twelve-month period.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.1. Duration. Unless this Agreement has been earlier terminated, Metavante shall provide a written renewal notice to Customer at least twelve (12) months prior to the expiration of the Initial Term (the "Renewal Notice"). Regardless of whether Customer notifies Metavante of its intent not to renew this Agreement in writing within a period of three (3) months following the Renewal Notice, this Agreement shall automatically renew at the end of the Initial Term on the same terms (including pricing terms) for one (1) twelve-month period.
perturbation.explanation: The original text outlines a process where the agreement auto-renews *unless* the customer opts out. The modified text removes the customer's ability to opt out, stating that the agreement auto-renews *regardless* of their notification. This is a in-text contradiction within the process for renewal, since the intention that the agreement gets renewed for the same term is uncertain
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.1. Duration. Unless this Agreement has been earlier terminated, Metavante shall provide a written renewal notice to Customer at least twelve (12) months prior to the expiration of the Initial Term (the "Renewal Notice"). Regardless of whether Customer notifies Metavante of its intent not to renew this Agreement in writing within a period of three (3) months following the Renewal Notice, this Agreement shall automatically renew at the end of the Initial Term on the same terms (including pricing terms) for one (1) twelve-month period. Upon expiration of such twelve (12) -month extension, this Agreement shall expire unless renewed in writing by the parties, provided, however, that Metavante may, but has no obligation to, continue to provide all or any portion of the Services thereafter on a month-to-month basis subject to these Terms and Conditions and Metavante's then-current standard fees and charges.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 2.1 removes the customer's right to opt out of automatic renewal, directly contradicting the original condition for renewal. The original text says it auto-renews *unless* the customer opts out, while the changed text says it auto-renews *regardless* of the customer's notification, which makes the expiration clause in the contradicted text uncertain. This constitutes a structural flaw leading to an in-text contradiction as the two sections conflict in intent, which fits the provided definition.

perturbation.original_text: Customer shall maintain adequate records for at least ten (10) Business Days, including backup on magnetic tape or other electronic media where transactions are being transmitted to Metavante, from which reconstruction of lost or damaged files or data can be made. Customer assumes all responsibility and liability for any loss or damage resulting from failure to maintain such records.
perturbation.changed_text: Customer shall maintain adequate records for at least ten (10) Business Days, including backup on magnetic tape or other electronic media where transactions are being transmitted to Metavante, from which reconstruction of lost or damaged files or data can be made. *Metavante* assumes all responsibility and liability for any loss or damage resulting from failure to maintain such records.
perturbation.explanation: The original text clearly places the responsibility and liability for loss or damage due to inadequate record-keeping on the *Customer*. The modification directly contradicts this by stating that *Metavante* assumes this responsibility. This in-text contradiction in terms of who holds the responsibility for losses due to inadequate records creates considerable confusion and uncertainty about the obligations of each party regarding data backup and recovery.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 16.2. Data Backup. Customer shall maintain adequate records for at least ten (10) Business Days, including backup on magnetic tape or other electronic media where transactions are being transmitted to Metavante, from which reconstruction of lost or damaged files or data can be made. Metavante assumes all responsibility and liability for any loss or damage resulting from failure to maintain such records.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text. The original text and the contradicted text state that the *Customer* assumes responsibility and liability for any loss or damage resulting from failure to maintain adequate records. The changed text assigns that responsibility to *Metavante*. This is a direct and irreconcilable conflict, creating a strong in-text contradiction.
